tree.setup: The school library, hushed and warm. Librarian Uncle Zhao sits behind a desk stacked with books he's been meaning to re-shelve for three weeks. He speaks in a whisper so reverent you'd think the books could hear him. A hand-painted sign reads: 'Silence is the music of knowledge.'
tree.setup_zh: 学校图书馆，安静又温暖。图书管理员赵叔叔坐在堆满书的桌子后面——那些书他打算上架已经三个星期了。他说话轻得像怕书能听见。一块手写的牌子写着：「安静是知识的音乐。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: xū... nǐ hǎo. nǐ yào zhǎo shénme shū?
turns.text_en: Shh... hello. What book are you looking for?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him what kind of book you need.

options.text_en: I'd like to find a Chinese book.
options.feedback: Uncle Zhao's eyes sparkle. He stands up with the urgency of someone who has waited years for this exact request. 找 (zhǎo, 'to look for') is one of the most useful verbs you'll learn.
Uncle Zhao gestures at the entire east wall with quiet pride. There are hundreds. He asks if you want easy ones or hard ones.「容易的还是难的？」
Uncle Zhao is unfazed. He produces a picture book about a cat who travels China and places it gently on the desk.「先看这个吧。」Even when you don't know what you want, librarians find a way.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: zhè běn shū hěn hǎo. nǐ kàn yíxià?
turns.text_en: This book is very good. Want to take a look?

options.feedback: Uncle Zhao hands over the book the way one passes a sleeping kitten. He whispers:「慢慢看。」本 (běn) is the measure word for books -- you'll see it everywhere.
Uncle Zhao stares at you with gentle horror. This is a library. The books are free to borrow. He recovers and points at the borrowing desk with trembling dignity.
Uncle Zhao shakes his head and opens it to the first page. Lots of pictures, big characters. He selected this with great care. 太...了吧 softens the complaint into a question.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 可以借一个星期。好好看！
turns.text_pinyin: kěyǐ jiè yí gè xīngqī. hǎohǎo kàn!
turns.text_en: You can borrow it for one week. Read it well!

tree.cultural_note: Chinese school and public libraries (图书馆, túshūguǎn) are often very quiet, hushed spaces. Librarians tend to take their role as guardians of knowledge seriously. 借 (jiè) means both 'to borrow' and 'to lend' -- context tells you which.
